column1: # Phase 2 — special-key tokens. `{NAME}` syntax in macros expands
# to the corresponding KeyEvent through the same dispatcher. A
# trailing integer is a repeat count (`{DOWN 5}` = five Downs).
# Function-key tokens — {GOTO} is F5, then keystrokes fill the prompt.
# `{TILDE}` types a literal `~` char into the entry buffer (vs `~`
# alone which is Enter). Together with `{LBRACE}`/`{RBRACE}` these
# are the macro-source escapes for the three metacharacters.
# {ESC} mid-entry cancels the entry. Pointer doesn't move.
# {CALC} == F9. We can't easily assert the CALC indicator (lights
# only in manual-recalc mode) but we can verify {CALC} doesn't
# {EDIT} == F2: opens EDIT mode on the current cell with the
# existing source in the buffer. {BS} twice + new digit edits the
